description: §52.15.5 (S255 auto-make-safe) — the SSR compose route is an ANONYMOUS-reachable GET, so an auth-scoped, non-row-scoped server-authority cell (Tier-1 `<Account authority=server table=users>` under `auth=required`) is AUTO-OMITTED from the first-paint: the E-ADAPTER SSR path invokes the REAL _scrml_ssr_compose_handler with an anonymous request and the composed HTML + `window.__scrml_ssr_state` seed contain NEITHER the users' rows (Alice/Bob) NOR an `accounts` seed key — they are absent by construction (the cell hydrates client-side behind its gated /__serverLoad fetch, 401 for anon). A SIBLING PUBLIC cell (`<widgets server auth=none> = ?{...}`) is NOT over-omitted: its rows ARE server-rendered into the first paint + carried in the seed, and hydrate in place. The compiler emits an I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED info lint (per-var, on @accounts) recording the omission, and NO E-* error (idiomatic code still compiles). This pins the route-admission × row-selection egress axis (§52.15.4), orthogonal to the §14.8.9 column-redaction floor.
rationale: §52.15.5 (auto-make-safe): 'an auth-scoped unscoped cell is OMITTED from the SSR seed — no first-paint markup fill, no window.__scrml_ssr_state entry. The cell then hydrates client-side behind its already-gated /__serverLoad fetch (401 for anon) — safe by construction.' The compose route is anonymous-reachable (§52.8), so seeding @accounts would leak every user's rows to an unauthenticated viewer (strictly worse than the gated client fetch). The public @widgets cell (auth=none → gate false) is SSR-seeded normally — the omission never over-omits.
